data.title: A whale closed its $2.4 million XPL short position half an hour ago and made a profit of $102,000. It then added some XPL positions.
data.description: According to monitoring by on-chain analyst Ai Yi, a whale account closed a short position in XPL worth $2.4 million half an hour ago and made a profit of $102,000. It then increased its XPL position again. Currently, the account has bought a total of 24.29 million XPL (about $26.22 million), with a floating profit of $3.26 million. The contract account still has $7.23 million in available funds.

data.title: "Big Brother Maji" XPL increased its five-fold leverage long position to $17.32 million, and currently holds 12 million tokens.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi’s monitoring, “Big Brother Maji” XPL’s five-fold leveraged long position increased by $17.32 million. The current position is 12 million, with an opening price of $1.52 and a floating loss of $1.023 million.

data.title: BlackRock Proposes Bitcoin Premium ETF Under 1933 Act
data.description: According to PANews, Bloomberg's senior ETF analyst Eric Balchunas highlighted on the X platform that BlackRock has filed for the iShares Bitcoin Premium ETF. This exchange-traded fund (ETF) is a spot product under the 1933 Act and employs a covered call strategy on Bitcoin. The aim is to generate some returns from Bitcoin investments. This ETF is an extension of another Bitcoin exchange-traded fund, IBIT. In the U.S. market, mainstream ETF products are required to comply with both the 1933 Act and the 1934 Act.

data.title: Hyperdrive suffers contract vulnerability attack, potentially losing $700,000
data.description: According to crypto influencer @CryptoNyaRu, Hyperdrive, a DeFi protocol within the Hyperliquid ecosystem, was attacked. The attack occurred because a user set the Router as an Operator during a lending process. However, the Router can execute calls on any whitelisted contract, and the Market contract is also whitelisted, allowing third-party addresses to manipulate user positions through the Router. The relevant contract addresses are Router (0x8D9e...) and Market (0xa522...). Exploited transactions have been reported, posing a risk to user asset security. According to @xhanTululu, Hyperdrive may have lost $700,000. The project has yet to respond.

data.title: Gavin Wood proposed that Polkadot's native stablecoin PUSD has entered the proposal discussion stage
data.description: Gavin Wood's proposal for Polkadot's native stablecoin, PUSD, has entered the discussion phase. His underlying concept is reportedly to replace the highly volatile DOT with block producer rewards, which could instead be issued in a stablecoin. Therefore, the Polkadot protocol itself (most likely on the Asset Hub) will issue a new stablecoin, called PUSD, collateralized solely by DOTs. Similar to the HOLLAR stablecoin, users can borrow PUSD by pledging DOTs. However, the community believes that a large-scale stablecoin would incur correlation risks. For example, a drop in the DOT price could trigger liquidations, which would necessitate the sale of DOTs held in the vault.

data.title: Hyperdrive Suspends Currency Markets for Investigation
data.description: According to PANews, Hyperdrive announced on the X platform that two accounts in the thBILL market have been compromised. As a precautionary measure, Hyperdrive has suspended all currency markets to conduct a thorough investigation. The issue does not affect HYPED.

data.title: BNB Surpasses 980 USDT with a 4.64% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Sep 27, 2025, 14:34 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has crossed the 980 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 980 USDT, with a narrowed 4.64% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: A newly created wallet withdrew a total of 1 billion MEME from Binance, worth nearly $2.35 million.
data.description: Arkham monitoring data showed that about seven minutes ago, a newly created wallet withdrew a total of 1 billion MEME tokens from the Binance hot wallet address through two transactions, worth approximately US$2.34711 million.

data.title: Polymarket predicts that the probability of the US government shutting down before October 1 has risen to 71%, with nearly $950,000 in bets.
data.description: Prediction market platform Polymarket shows that the probability of a US government shutdown before October 1st has risen to 71%, a 17% increase from the previous week. Cumulative trading volume in the market is close to $950,000. Related markets have priced the probability of a government shutdown throughout 2025 at 82%. Previous reports have indicated that a US government shutdown would create a data gap for the Federal Reserve's interest rate meeting.

data.title: Bitcoin Treasury Company POP Culture Plans to Raise $6 Million in a Rights Issue
data.description: POP Culture, a Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin treasury company, announced plans to sell 5,000,000 shares of its Class A common stock at $1.20 per share, raising an estimated $6 million. The rights offering is expected to close on or about September 29, 2025, subject to customary closing conditions. The company has previously purchased 300 Bitcoins and plans to increase its Bitcoin treasury to 1,000. (PR Newswire)

data.title: BNY Mellon and BNP Paribas have begun testing Ethereum L2 on-chain messaging with SWIFT.
data.description: The Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication (SWIFT) previously disclosed that it is using Ethereum's Layer 2 network, Linea, to test on-chain messaging capabilities. According to the latest news, BNP Paribas and Bank of New York Mellon (BNY) have begun participating in the test, and the project currently involves "more than a dozen financial institutions." An unnamed source said that the full testing is expected to take several months to complete, but it will bring a significant technological transformation to the international interbank payment industry. (FinanceFeeds)

data.title: POP Culture Plans $6 Million Stock Offering to Expand Bitcoin Holdings
data.description: According to PANews, Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin treasury firm POP Culture has announced plans to sell 5,000,000 shares of Class A common stock at a price of $1.20 per share. The company aims to raise approximately $6 million through this offering, which is expected to be completed around September 29, 2025, pending customary closing conditions. Previously, POP Culture acquired 300 Bitcoins and intends to expand its Bitcoin treasury to 1,000 coins.
